name: Stripe
description: The de-facto standard for indie payment processing. Best-in-class docs, instant payouts, global coverage. Higher fees than competitors but saves weeks of integration work.
bestFor: Any solo founder accepting online payments — best docs and ecosystem in the industry
skipIf: You need built-in global tax compliance on day one (try Lemon Squeezy instead)

name: Lemon Squeezy
tagline: Merchant of Record for software companies
description: Handles global tax compliance so you don't have to. Ideal for solo founders selling digital products or SaaS who want zero VAT headaches. Slightly higher fees than Stripe but saves hours of compliance work.
bestFor: Solo founders selling digital products or SaaS globally who want zero VAT headaches
skipIf: You need physical product shipping or complex B2B pricing structures

name: Paddle
description: Merchant of Record with stronger subscription tooling than Lemon Squeezy. Better for higher-volume SaaS. More enterprise-grade but overkill for early-stage.
bestFor: SaaS founders with $10K+ MRR who need enterprise-grade subscription management
skipIf: You're pre-revenue — Lemon Squeezy is simpler and cheaper at early stage

name: Plausible
description: Lightweight script under 1KB, GDPR-compliant by default, no cookie banner needed. Solo-founder favourite — one dashboard with everything you actually need.
bestFor: Founders who want clean, actionable page analytics without GDPR cookie banner complexity
skipIf: You need session replay or deep funnel/cohort analysis — PostHog does more

name: Fathom Analytics
description: GDPR/CCPA compliant. Slightly pricier than Plausible but has a 25% recurring affiliate program — very popular in the indie community for that reason.
bestFor: Founders who want to earn from affiliates AND get privacy-first analytics in one tool
skipIf: Budget is tight — Plausible is $5/mo cheaper with very similar features

name: Vercel
description: Zero-config deployment for Next.js and any frontend framework. Free tier is generous. GitHub push to live in 30 seconds. The default choice for solo founders shipping fast.
bestFor: Next.js projects and any frontend needing fast global deployment with zero config
skipIf: You're running persistent servers or heavy backend workloads — use Railway instead

name: Netlify
tagline: The fastest way to build and deploy websites
description: Strong alternative to Vercel, especially for non-Next.js projects. Excellent form handling and serverless functions. Free tier is generous.
bestFor: Static sites, Gatsby, and non-Next.js projects needing built-in form handling
skipIf: You're building a Next.js app — Vercel has better native Next.js integration

name: Railway
description: The easiest way to run a backend or database. No Kubernetes, no YAML hell. Deploy from GitHub in minutes. Postgres, Redis, MySQL all one-click.
bestFor: Backend services, APIs, and databases with GitHub deploy and no infrastructure YAML
skipIf: You need a free tier — Railway charges from day one (use Neon for free Postgres)

name: Supabase
description: Postgres + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions. The default backend stack for solo founders who want to move fast. Free tier is very generous for early products.
bestFor: Full-stack apps needing auth + Postgres + file storage + edge functions in one place
skipIf: You need only a database — Neon gives you serverless Postgres at a lower price

name: Cursor
description: VSCode fork with deep AI integration. Tab-completion, multi-file editing, natural language diffs. The most adopted AI coding tool among indie founders in 2026.
bestFor: Solo devs who want AI-assisted coding throughout the day including multi-file edits
skipIf: You're not writing much code daily — the $20/mo subscription isn't worth it for occasional use

name: GitHub Copilot
description: Deepest IDE integration across all editors. Best for inline completions. Less capable than Cursor for multi-file refactors but more stable.
bestFor: Developers already in VS Code or JetBrains who want inline AI completions without switching editors
skipIf: You want multi-file AI editing and context-aware refactors — Cursor is better at that

name: Beehiiv
description: Best newsletter platform for monetization-focused builders. Built-in referral program, boosts, ad network. Free tier up to 2,500 subscribers.
bestFor: Founders building a newsletter as a primary growth and monetization channel
skipIf: You just want transactional email for your product — use Resend, not Beehiiv

name: Typefully
tagline: Write and schedule Twitter threads that grow
description: The best tool for solo founders who use Twitter/X for distribution. Thread composer, scheduling, analytics. Many indie hackers swear by it for building in public.
bestFor: Solo founders building in public on X who want better thread tools and post scheduling
skipIf: Your audience isn't on X or you post rarely — not worth the subscription

name: Resend
description: The cleanest transactional email API. React Email component library, excellent deliverability, generous free tier of 3,000 emails/mo. The new default for indie SaaS.
bestFor: Developers building transactional email (receipts, onboarding, alerts) into their product
skipIf: You need a marketing newsletter platform — use Beehiiv or ConvertKit for that

name: Postmark
description: Industry-leading deliverability. Best choice when email reliability is mission-critical. Pricier than Resend but more mature with better inbox rates.
bestFor: Apps where email delivery is mission-critical (receipts, auth codes, financial alerts)
skipIf: You're pre-launch or budget-conscious — Resend has a better free tier and similar quality

name: Crisp
description: Live chat + inbox + knowledge base + chatbot. Free tier for 2 seats is very usable for solo founders. Replaces Intercom at 1/10th the cost.
bestFor: Products with repeat customer questions or onboarding friction that needs a live chat widget
skipIf: You don't have enough traffic yet — live chat is overkill under 100 daily active users

name: Tally
description: Notion-like form builder. Free tier is remarkably generous — unlimited forms, submissions, file uploads. Perfect for feedback forms, user research, support tickets.
bestFor: Waitlists, feedback forms, user research, and support intake with zero dev work required
skipIf: You need advanced conditional logic or calculated fields — Typeform handles that better

name: Clerky
description: Automated Delaware C-Corp formation, SAFEs, NDAs. Used by YC founders. Cheaper than a lawyer, more reliable than LegalZoom. Essential if you plan to raise.
bestFor: Founders planning to raise from VCs who need a clean Delaware C-Corp and SAFEs done right
skipIf: You're building a lifestyle business or don't plan to raise — Stripe Atlas is $300 cheaper

name: Termly
tagline: Privacy policy and compliance made easy
description: Auto-generate GDPR/CCPA compliant privacy policies, terms of service, cookie banners. Free tier covers most early-stage needs.
bestFor: Any site collecting user data, emails, or payments — you need this before launch
skipIf: You only need a one-time document — Termly is a subscription for hosted live policies

name: Figma
description: The standard design tool. Free tier is sufficient for solo founders. Massive template library. Figma-to-code plugins now make design-to-implementation faster than ever.
bestFor: Founders who need to design product UI, wireframes, or prototypes to share with others
skipIf: You just need marketing graphics or social posts — Canva is faster for that

name: Canva
description: Marketing graphics, social media posts, presentations, logos. Faster than Figma for non-UI work. Free tier is excellent.
bestFor: Non-designers creating social posts, ads, pitch decks, and brand assets quickly
skipIf: You're designing actual product UI — Figma gives you the precision needed

name: Linear
tagline: The issue tracker for high-performance teams
description: Fastest issue tracker. Even better solo — keyboard-first, lightning speed, clean design. Free tier. Many indie founders switched from Notion to Linear for task management.
bestFor: Developer-founders who want a keyboard-first, blazing-fast issue tracker with zero bloat
skipIf: You're non-technical and want a flexible workspace for notes + tasks — Notion does both

name: Notion
tagline: The connected workspace for your notes and docs
description: All-in-one workspace: notes, docs, databases, wikis. Many solo founders run their entire business in Notion. Slower than Linear for task tracking but far more flexible.
bestFor: Founders who want one tool for docs, notes, roadmap, CRM, and content in a single workspace
skipIf: You want fast task management — Linear is 10x faster and less cognitively cluttered

name: Wise Business
tagline: International payments at real exchange rates
description: Send, receive, and hold 50+ currencies at mid-market rates. Best for indie founders with international customers or contractors. Far cheaper than PayPal for cross-border payments.
bestFor: Founders with international clients, contractors, or suppliers who want real exchange rates
skipIf: All your transactions are USD within the US — a regular business bank account is enough

name: Cloudflare Pages
tagline: Full-stack deployment on Cloudflare's edge network
description: Free tier is extraordinary: unlimited bandwidth, unlimited sites, unlimited builds. Fastest global CDN. The best free alternative to Vercel for high-traffic static sites.
bestFor: High-traffic static sites that need unlimited free bandwidth and the world's best CDN
skipIf: You're using Next.js server components — Vercel has better framework-level support

name: PostHog
description: Product analytics + session replay + feature flags + A/B testing in one tool. Open source and self-hostable. Free tier is generous (1M events/month). Popular with solo SaaS founders who want depth beyond page views.
bestFor: SaaS founders who need product funnels, feature flags, and session replay beyond page views
skipIf: You just need page view analytics — Plausible is simpler and much faster to set up

name: Cal.com
description: Calendly alternative that's open source and self-hostable. Free tier includes unlimited event types and bookings. Perfect for solo founders doing sales calls, demos, or onboarding sessions.
bestFor: Founders doing sales calls, demos, or consulting who want a free Calendly alternative
skipIf: You rarely book calls or already have Calendly embedded in your existing workflow

name: Clerk
tagline: Authentication and user management built for developers
description: Drop-in auth with pre-built UI components for sign-up, sign-in, and user profile. Works with Next.js, React, and most frameworks. Free tier supports up to 10,000 monthly active users — more than enough to get to revenue.
bestFor: Next.js and React founders who want production-ready auth UI without building it themselves
skipIf: You need full control over auth internals or are using a non-JS backend

name: Auth.js
tagline: Open source authentication for the web
description: The most-used auth library for Next.js (formerly NextAuth). Supports 80+ OAuth providers, email/password, and database sessions. Fully self-hosted and free — you own all your user data.
bestFor: Developers who want flexible, self-hosted auth with full data ownership in Next.js
skipIf: You want a pre-built login UI — Auth.js requires you to build the UI yourself

name: Make
description: The most powerful visual automation builder. Handles complex multi-step workflows with branching, data transformation, and 2,000+ app integrations. Free tier includes 1,000 operations/month.
bestFor: Non-developers building complex visual workflows between apps with branching logic
skipIf: You want self-hosted or Git-versioned automation — use n8n for that

name: n8n
tagline: Open source workflow automation you can self-host
description: Developer-friendly visual automation with 400+ integrations. Self-host on Railway or any VPS for free, or use n8n Cloud. Full code execution nodes let you run JavaScript inline.
bestFor: Technical founders who want open-source, self-hosted automation with full code execution
skipIf: You're non-technical and want a simpler drag-and-drop experience — Make is more approachable

name: Zapier
description: The most reliable and trusted automation platform with 7,000+ app integrations. Best for simple 2-step automations where reliability matters. Pricing gets steep fast as you scale.
bestFor: Non-technical founders who need reliable simple automations between well-known SaaS tools
skipIf: You need complex multi-step logic — Make offers better visual branching at a lower price

name: Microsoft Clarity
tagline: Free heatmaps and session replay for any website
description: 100% free heatmaps, session recordings, and user behaviour analytics. No traffic limits, no time limits. Owned by Microsoft so it's going nowhere. The obvious first choice for budget-conscious founders.
bestFor: Any site wanting free heatmaps and session replay to see exactly how users behave
skipIf: You need advanced segmentation or funnel analysis — PostHog or Hotjar cover that

name: Hotjar
tagline: Heatmaps, recordings, and surveys in one tool
description: The original user behaviour analytics tool. Heatmaps, session recordings, on-site surveys, and user interviews. Slightly pricier than alternatives but deeply integrated and trusted.
bestFor: UX-focused founders who want heatmaps plus in-product surveys and user interview scheduling
skipIf: You're budget-conscious — Microsoft Clarity gives heatmaps + session replay for free

name: LogSnag
tagline: Real-time event tracking for your product
description: Track custom business events as a real-time feed. New user signup? Payment received? Error spike? Get a notification and a filterable feed. Think of it as Slack for your product events.
bestFor: Developers who want a real-time feed of business events without building their own alerting
skipIf: You need full product analytics with funnels — PostHog covers much more

name: Neon
description: Serverless Postgres that scales to zero (free when idle). Git-like database branching lets you test schema changes safely. Free tier includes 3GB storage — excellent for early-stage apps.
bestFor: Postgres projects that benefit from serverless scaling-to-zero and safe database branching
skipIf: You need Redis, queues, or non-Postgres databases alongside your backend

name: Fly.io
tagline: Run your full-stack app close to your users
description: Deploy Docker containers to 34 global regions in minutes. Supports any language or framework — Go, Python, Node, Elixir. Free tier includes 3 small VMs and 3GB persistent storage.
bestFor: Developers deploying full-stack apps in any language who need global edge distribution
skipIf: You're deploying a frontend-only Next.js app — Vercel is far simpler for that

name: PocketBase
description: Open source backend with a built-in realtime database, user auth, file storage, and admin UI. Deploy it as a single binary. Zero dependencies, zero configuration. Perfect for side projects and MVPs.
bestFor: Developers who want an entire backend as a single Go binary with zero vendor lock-in
skipIf: You need a fully managed cloud service with no servers to maintain — use Supabase instead

name: GitHub Actions
description: Free CI/CD built directly into GitHub. Run tests, lint, build, and deploy on every push. Free tier includes 2,000 minutes/month — enough for most solo projects. No separate CI tool needed.
bestFor: Any GitHub-hosted project needing free CI/CD without adding another tool to the stack
skipIf: You're deploying to Vercel or Railway — those already have native GitHub push-to-deploy

name: Cloudflare
tagline: DNS, DDoS protection, and CDN for free
description: The world's best free CDN with DDoS protection, DNS management, SSL, and edge computing via Workers. Free tier is absurdly generous. Every serious website should be behind Cloudflare.
bestFor: Any public website needing DDoS protection, global CDN, and free SSL in under 5 minutes
skipIf: You're just getting started and not yet public — add it once you have real traffic

name: Ghost
description: Open source CMS with a built-in newsletter, paid memberships, and SEO optimization. Ghost Pro is managed hosting. Self-host on Railway for free. Popular with founder-led content businesses.
bestFor: Founders building a content business with blog + newsletter + paid memberships in one platform
skipIf: You already use Beehiiv for newsletters — Ghost adds complexity without much gain

name: Kit (ConvertKit)
description: The go-to email platform for creators, bloggers, and course sellers. Visual automation builder, tag-based segmentation, landing pages included. Free tier up to 1,000 subscribers.
bestFor: Content-first founders and creators building an email list with automation sequences
skipIf: You're product-focused, not content-focused — Beehiiv has better growth mechanics

name: Framer
tagline: Build stunning websites visually with AI
description: Visual website builder with AI generation, animations, and CMS. Best-looking no-code websites on the internet. Free tier includes one published site. The choice of solo founders who care deeply about design.
bestFor: Founders who want a polished marketing site or portfolio with animations without writing HTML
skipIf: You need a web app with user auth or dynamic data — Framer is for static marketing sites

name: Webflow
description: Visual web development platform with a built-in CMS. More powerful than Framer for content-heavy sites. Steeper learning curve but enables fully custom designs that look hand-coded.
bestFor: Design-heavy marketing sites that need a built-in CMS for blog or content management
skipIf: You're comfortable with code — Next.js + Tailwind gives more control with less vendor lock-in

name: Windsurf
description: VSCode-based AI editor with Cascade — a multi-file AI agent that can browse your codebase, run commands, and write code across files. Strong Cursor competitor at a lower starting price.
bestFor: Developers who want Cursor-like multi-file AI editing at a lower monthly price point
skipIf: You're already on Cursor Pro and happy with it — switching costs outweigh the savings

name: v0 by Vercel
tagline: Generate UI from text with AI
description: Generate full React component code from a text prompt. Produces clean Tailwind + shadcn/ui components ready to copy into your project. Best for rapid UI prototyping when you know what you want but not how to build it.
bestFor: Rapid UI prototyping — generate React/Tailwind components from a description in seconds
skipIf: You need backend code, business logic, or production-hardened components — it's UI only

name: Obsidian
description: Local-first markdown knowledge base with a graph view. Your notes are plain .md files on your computer — no vendor, no cloud dependency. Massive plugin ecosystem. Popular with builders who do deep research.
bestFor: Solo founders who want a local-first second brain for research, notes, and knowledge management
skipIf: You need to share notes with collaborators — Notion or a hosted tool is better for that

name: Height
description: Height combines tasks, docs, threads, and chat in one async workspace. Think of it as a Notion + Linear hybrid. Good for solo founders who want to reduce context-switching between planning and execution.
bestFor: Async-focused founders who want tasks + docs + discussions combined without switching tools
skipIf: You want keyboard-first speed in task management — Linear wins decisively there

name: Plain
tagline: Modern customer support built for developers
description: Slack-like support inbox with full API control, thread-based conversations, and powerful labels. Built for developer tools and B2B SaaS. Fast, opinionated, and much cheaper than Intercom.
bestFor: Developer tools or B2B SaaS founders who need a modern support inbox with deep API integration
skipIf: You're B2C with high volume — Plain is priced for quality support, not mass scale

name: Senja
tagline: Collect and display testimonials on autopilot
description: Automated testimonial collection via email or link. Import from Twitter, Product Hunt, G2. Embed as widgets on your landing page in minutes. A conversion tool disguised as a support tool.
bestFor: Founders who want to collect and display testimonials automatically to boost conversion
skipIf: You don't yet have users or customers — collect some wins first

name: Stripe Atlas
tagline: Incorporate your startup online in minutes
description: Delaware C-Corp incorporation from Stripe. Includes a Stripe account, a US bank account, and basic legal docs. Used by thousands of international founders building US-based companies. Simpler than Clerky but less feature-rich.
bestFor: International founders who need a US C-Corp fast with a bundled Stripe account and bank account
skipIf: You're a US-based founder who needs complex cap table management — use Clerky instead
